rationale: The design brief is well-structured and implementation-feasible. The three-track decomposition (naming, code cleanup, release) correctly orders dependencies. I verified the codebase state: 14 agent files exist with hyphenated names, plugin.json lists all 14 with hyphenated paths, 154 hyphenated references exist across 32 .md files and 21 across 7 .json files. The evolution.ts cwd bug is confirmed -- both gap-assessment (line 42) and rules-update (line 86) call writeJsonFile without passing { cwd }, causing post-write checks to fall back to process.cwd() instead of the resolved working directory. The mission.ts duplicate readInputData (lines 16-44) is confirmed as a local reimplementation that lacks --file support compared to lib/input.ts. The v3 no-op functions (checkMemoryPromotion, checkStaleMemoryRefs) are confirmed at post-write-checks.ts lines 381-405 with dispatcher call sites at lines 533 and 536. Typecheck passes clean. All identified bugs and cleanup targets are real and well-scoped.
concerns: Version mapping gap: plugin.json currently shows 2.1.0, not 2.0.1. The tag mapping in the brief covers v0.2.0 through v2.0.1 (8 tags) but HEAD is at 2.1.0 (untagged). The brief says HEAD continues as v0.5.1, but this skips accounting for the 2.0.1->2.1.0 gap. The task should explicitly document that current untagged HEAD (2.1.0) maps to v0.5.1 and no tag is created for 2.1.0 since it was never tagged.

No test script exists in the CLI package (npm test yields 'Missing script: test'). The acceptance criterion 'npm test passes' cannot currently be verified. The test files exist under tests/ but there is no test runner configured in package.json. This needs to be reconciled -- either the acceptance criterion should reference the actual test command (e.g., npx vitest or however tests are actually run), or a test script needs to be added.

Removing v3 no-op functions (checkMemoryPromotion, checkStaleMemoryRefs) requires updating both the function exports AND the dispatcher call sites in runPostWriteChecks (lines 533, 536) AND the module header comment (lines 9-10). Missing any of these three locations would cause build failure or stale documentation.

The rename of 14 agent files on Windows requires care with git case sensitivity. Git on Windows is case-insensitive by default (core.ignorecase=true), so renaming from hyphens to underscores should work fine since the character change is significant. However, the rename and plugin.json update must be in the same commit to avoid a broken intermediate state where the plugin cannot discover agents.

The scope of hyphenated references is larger than it might appear: 32 .md files with 154 occurrences plus 7 .json files with 21 occurrences. Some of these are inside agent .md files themselves (self-references in frontmatter/content), which will naturally change with the file rename but still need content updates.

Korean documentation (docs/ko/) has parallel files: AGENTS.md (33 occurrences), HOOKS.md (1), DESIGN.md (4), protocol/09 (4), and example files. The mission spec lists Korean docs as out-of-scope, but the design review correctly notes that docs/ko/protocol/INDEX.md must be updated to v1. The affected_surfaces in the spec already includes docs/ko/protocol/INDEX.md, but the broader Korean reference updates for agent names should be clarified -- are they in or out?
